metadata.title: Sample Parliamentary Debate Round — Renewable Energy

segments.text: The government side contends that renewable energy subsidies are essential to combating climate change.
segments.speaker: GOV1

segments.text: Fossil fuels currently account for over 80 percent of global energy supply, and without policy intervention that share will not fall fast enough.
segments.speaker: GOV1

segments.text: Therefore, direct subsidies to solar and wind producers create the price signal that private markets have failed to provide.
segments.speaker: GOV1

segments.text: The opposition disagrees. Subsidies distort market competition and prop up inefficient producers at taxpayer expense.
segments.speaker: OPP1

segments.text: The evidence shows that renewable costs have dropped 90 percent in a decade without sustained subsidy — the market is already working.
segments.speaker: OPP1

segments.text: Government intervention at this stage risks locking in today's technology and crowding out the breakthrough innovations we actually need.
segments.speaker: OPP1

segments.text: A point of information: does the opposition accept that without a carbon price, the externality remains unpriced regardless of cost curves?
segments.speaker: GOV2
